title: alnus glutinosa f. parvifolia
intro: Alnus glutinosa f. parvifolia, also known as dwarf alder or small-leaved alder, is a variant of the alder tree species, characterized by its smaller leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Alnus glutinosa f. parvifolia include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
